mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion and rising consumer income, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of July 2013, PAYEMS at 136,038k showing post-recession recovery trajectory
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 1.5% in a low-rate environment, it signals the Fed is tightening monetary policy, which increases discount rates on future corporate cash flows and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of July 2013, indicating sustained accommodative policy
regime_hint: monetary tightening initiation from low-rate regime
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns fail to materialize in > 65% of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.5%, or the mechanism produces positive returns in more than 50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strengthening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, jointly driving equity prices upward over 20 trading days through improved earnings growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 836k and RSAFS at 424,481M as of June-July 2013
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand acceleration
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of months where both HOUST and RSAFS show month-over-month growth, or the joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 3-month moving average signals weakening supply-side capacity utilization and potential stagflationary pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances where INDPRO breaches its 3-month MA, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.4% as of July 2013, elevated from post-crisis lows
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns fail to materialize in > 58% of triggered instances, or the mechanism produces positive returns in more than 48% of cases where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1%, it signals monetary policy is falling behind inflation dynamics, creating real rate compression that initially supports equity valuations but signals future tightening risk, producing mixed 20-day returns conditional on market expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 233.318 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of July 2013
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with emerging inflation pressure
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered periods, or the interaction term produces lower predictive power (Rank IC) than FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15,648.7B (chained 2017 dollars) as of Q1 2013
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 62% of quarters where GDPC1 QoQ growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates while unemployment (UNRATE) remains elevated above 7%, it signals a supply-constrained recovery with limited labor slack, creating stagflationary conditions that compress equity valuations and produce negative 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.9462 and UNRATE at 7.4% as of July 2013
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure with constrained supply and slack labor market
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or the joint interaction signal produces lower Rank IC than either INDPRO or UNRATE independently.
